display.purpose: Hero statement only. Spectral 300; the only place thin weight is allowed.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, tags, numerals. Barlow Condensed 600 uppercase, champagne, <=4 words.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Ease-out-quint. The only curve in the system; no bounce or elastic.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The close. Champagne fill, navy text. Carries the heat; used once per view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #c2a368; color: #0a1622; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 16px 32px; border: none; border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #d8bf8a; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d8bf8a; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(0); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Lower-priority action. Navy surface, cream text, champagne hairline. Keeps the primary singular.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: #0a1622; color: #e7e6e0; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 16px 32px; border: 1px solid rgba(194,163,104,0.22); border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #c2a368; color: #fbfbf8; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #d8bf8a; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Tag
components.description: Champagne fill, navy text. Market or solution label.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; background: #c2a368; color: #0a1622; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.72rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Finding Card
components.description: A single gap finding. Navy-card surface, hairline border, lifts on hover via tonal step, no shadow.
components.html:
07

Power Quality — full coverage

Without harmonic distortion management at 85–90% nonlinear load, you cannot credibly serve Data Centers.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0c1a28; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border-radius: 0; padding: 32px; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-card:hover { background: #16293b; border-color: rgba(194,163,104,0.22); } .ds-card-num { font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #c2a368; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1.5rem; line-height: 1.32; color: #fbfbf8; margin: 8px 0 12px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Barlow', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 400; font-size: 1.0625rem; line-height: 1.65; color: #e7e6e0; margin: 0; max-width: 65ch; }

components.name: Audit Option
components.description: A selectable answer in the 16-question flow. Hairline-defined, champagne marks selection. No heavy fill (avoids the quiz look).
components.css: .ds-option { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; background: #0c1a28; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border-radius: 2px; padding: 16px 20px; color: #e7e6e0; font-family: 'Barlow', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.0625rem; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-option:hover { border-color: rgba(194,163,104,0.5); } .ds-option:focus-within { outline: 2px solid #d8bf8a; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-option-input { accent-color: #c2a368; width: 18px; height: 18px; } .ds-option:has(.ds-option-input:checked) { border-color: #c2a368; color: #fbfbf8; }

components.name: With / Without Row
components.description: Signature component. The only place green and red appear: sober, tabular, sting-becomes-relief made literal.
components.html:
WITHOUT

Locked out of Healthcare & Data Centers.

WITH ENERGY+

Credible across both, plus 179D access.
components.css: .ds-compare { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); } .ds-compare-side { background: #0c1a28; padding: 24px; } .ds-compare-side p { font-family: 'Barlow', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.0625rem; line-height: 1.65; color: #e7e6e0; margin: 8px 0 0; } .ds-compare-label { font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.72rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em; } .ds-without .ds-compare-label { color: #d6796d; } .ds-with .ds-compare-label { color: #57bf98; }

narrative.overview: A genuine diagnostic that happens to close a sale. The surface reads like a serious deliverable an operator would respect: a deep-navy field of charged information, hairline-ruled and numbered like a real coverage report, warmed only by champagne accent and Spectral serif. Sharp and consultative first; warm enough in the findings that a gap lands as an invitation, never a verdict. Warmth comes from the champagne accent and the serif type, never from a tinted background. The heat is rationed so it always means something. The visuals serve a two-beat arc: a small sting, then immediate relief on the same screen.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-navy surface, champagne as the single rationed accent
Spectral serif for display and headings; Barlow for body, Barlow Condensed for labels and numerals
Hairline rules and a numbered/index feel borrowed from real audit reports
WCAG AA contrast as a floor, not a target
Motion that confirms and reveals, never performs

rules.name: The Rationed Heat Rule
rules.body: Champagne is the only accent and is used sparingly. If it is doing more than ~10% of a screen, cut it back.

rules.name: The Filled-Accent-Goes-Dark Rule
rules.body: Anything filled with champagne takes navy dark text (~6.5:1). White text on champagne is forbidden.

rules.name: The No-Dimmer-Grey Rule
rules.body: --muted (#8597a6) is the floor. Never introduce a greyer text color on navy.

rules.name: The Comparison-Only Status Rule
rules.body: Green and red appear only in the with/without comparison. Never decorative.

rules.name: The 400-Floor Rule
rules.body: Body weight is 400, minimum 15px. Weight 300 is reserved for large Spectral display only.

rules.name: The Serif-Is-The-Voice Rule
rules.body: Spectral carries every display and heading; Barlow never sets a headline; Spectral never sets running body.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Layer with the navy ramp and hairlines, not shadows.

dos: Do keep the background deep navy and let warmth come from champagne accent + Spectral serif only.
Do ration champagne to <=~10% of any screen; it is the heat.
Do give every champagne-filled element dark navy text (~6.5:1).
Do set body at weight 400, 15px minimum; reserve weight 300 for large Spectral display.
Do keep text contrast within the ramp: body --cream (~14:1), leads --mid (~9:1), supporting --muted (~5.5:1).
Do use --ease-out (ease-out-quint) as the only curve; ~100-150ms micro, 200-450ms reveals.
Do ship one orchestrated hero entrance and stagger list reveals (--i * 55ms); gate reveals behind a .js class so content shows if JS fails.
Do include a prefers-reduced-motion block that zeroes durations and reveals all content.
Do restrict imagery to commercial/industrial infrastructure and schematics, duotone or grayscale-warm.
donts: Don't use a cream, beige, sand, or tinted near-white background.
Don't use yellow-gold; champagne only. No gradient text. No glassmorphism by default.
Don't put a tracked-uppercase eyebrow above every section, or numbered markers as section scaffolding; numbers are only for a real sequence or catalog index.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored side-stripe accent.
Don't add quiz theatrics: no score-reveal animation, confetti, or playful illustration.
Don't add popups, countdowns, or gated walls; the CTA is earned by the findings.
Don't let it read as sterile corporate navy-and-gray.
Don't use Cormorant Garamond or emoji as icons.
Don't use sunsets, leaves, residential-roof installers, handshakes, or SaaS 3D blobs.
Don't introduce a grey dimmer than --muted, or white text on champagne.
Don't use green/red anywhere but the with/without comparison.
